description: Prevent execution of unapproved/malicious programs, including executables, software libraries, scripts, and installer files.

requirements.text: Application control is implemented on workstations to restrict the execution of executables, software libraries, scripts, and installers to an approved set.
Application control is implemented on workstations and servers to restrict execution to an approved set.
Application control rulesets are reviewed at least annually to ensure only currently required software is allowed to execute.
Application control is implemented on workstations and servers. Microsoft's recommended block rules are enabled and updated monthly.

description: Remediate security vulnerabilities in applications. Use vendor-supported versions.

requirements.text: Security vulnerabilities in applications are patched within 2 weeks of release, or within 48 hours if an active exploit is reported.
Web browsers, email clients, office suites, and PDF software are patched or updated within 2 weeks (or 48 hours for active exploits).
All application security vulnerabilities are scanned daily, and vendor support status is audited monthly.

description: Block execution of macros from untrusted sources, particularly files downloaded from the internet.

requirements.text: Microsoft Office macros are disabled for users who do not have a demonstrated business need.
Macros in files from the internet are blocked from executing. Only digitally signed macros or macros from trusted locations are allowed.
expectedEvidence: GPO settings for macro execution block

requirements.text: Macro execution is blocked unless signed by trusted publishers. Macro security settings cannot be bypassed by users.

description: Configure web browsers and other common user applications to block flash, java, ads, and unapproved extensions.

requirements.text: Web browsers are configured to block Flash, Java, and other deprecated web plugins.
Microsoft Office applications are configured to block activation of OLE packages, Flash, and active content.
expectedEvidence: Registry exports for Office trust settings

requirements.text: Web browsers block third-party extensions and advertising unless explicitly whitelisted by administration.
expectedEvidence: Web filter and ad blocker deployment status

description: Restrict access to administrative privileges. Do not use admin accounts for reading email or web browsing.

requirements.text: Requests for administrative privileges are validated, and privileged accounts do not have access to email or web browsing.
Administrative privileges are audited at least every six months. Local administrator accounts are disabled or managed via a solution like LAPS.
Just-In-Time (JIT) access and short-lived administrative credentials are used for all admin tasks.

description: Patch vulnerabilities in operating systems of workstations, servers, and network devices.

requirements.text: Workstation and server operating systems are patched within 2 weeks of vendor release, or 48 hours for active exploits.
Operating systems on network devices are patched within 2 weeks of vendor release, or 48 hours for active exploits.
Daily checking for new OS updates is automated, and unsupported OS versions are decommissioned/segregated.

description: Implement multi-factor authentication for all users, especially for administrative accounts and remote access.

requirements.text: Multi-factor authentication is used for all administrative access and remote access.
expectedEvidence: Entra ID conditional access policy export

requirements.text: MFA is implemented for all users accessing web portals, cloud services, and collaboration platforms.
expectedEvidence: MFA status report for all users

requirements.text: MFA is phishing-resistant (e.g. FIDO2 / Windows Hello for Business) and is enforced for all systems.

description: Perform regular backups of data, applications, and settings. Store backups offline or in a segregated manner.

requirements.text: Backups of critical data, applications, and settings are performed at least weekly.
Backups are stored in an offline or segregated manner, and restore tests are conducted at least annually.
Backups are protected with write-once-read-many (WORM) storage or immutable policies, and restore tests are conducted at least quarterly.
